title: The Rebellion of the People of Palestine and Jordan and the Treatment They Received from Yazid b. al-Walid
description: Jordan and the Treatment They Received from Yazid b. al-Wald According to Ahmad (b. Zuhayr)-'Ali b. Muhammad (al-Mada'ini)-'Amr b. Marwan al-Kalbi -Raja' b. Rawb b. Salamah b. Rawb b. Zinba':965 Said b. 'Abd al-Malik, a man of good character, was al-Walid's governor in Palestine. Yazid b. Sulayman (b. 'Abd al-Malik) was the chief of his father's sons966 They had been 962. Al-Khadrd': the palace built at Damascus by Mu'iwiyah, the first Umayyad caliph.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 26
